workflow_description: A personalized Rich Snippet Booster that turns a single domain input into: (1) best-fit rich snippet recommendations with copy/paste JSON-LD, (2) conservative CTR uplift estimates, (3) SERP mockup images, and (4) a premium interactive HTML report with before/after comparisons and implementation checklist. 2-step pipeline: structured research via web search, then HTML deliverable with generated SERP mockups.

steps.step_description: Crawls the target domain via web search, inventories key pages, selects top 5 schema types, generates copy/paste JSON-LD for each, estimates CTR uplift, and scores AI visibility. Outputs strict JSON.
steps.instructions:
Act as a Senior Technical SEO Strategist and Schema Engineer.

## Input
Use all available form fields:
- [domain] (required) — the website to analyze
- [business_type] (optional) — e.g. SaaS, ecommerce, local, publisher, course creator
- [top_country] (optional) — primary audience geography
- [cms] (optional) — WordPress, Webflow, Shopify, Kajabi, etc.

## Task Overview
Conduct a comprehensive domain analysis and produce a complete rich snippet strategy with copy/paste JSON-LD schemas. You must fill every field in the output schema with real, evidence-backed data.

## Phase 1: Domain Reconnaissance
1. Normalize the domain: strip protocol/path/query, keep root host.
2. Determine canonical_base_url (https://[domain]/ vs https://www.[domain]/).
3. Discover 8-15 key URLs: homepage, pricing/plans, product/course pages, blog index + 2-3 posts, FAQ page, about/contact, sitemap.
4. For each URL, extract visible evidence:
   - Page title (or SERP title)
   - Short summary of what the page offers
   - Price evidence (exact text + currency if visible)
   - Rating/review evidence (exact text + count if visible)
   - FAQ evidence (question/answer pairs if visible)
   - Video evidence (YouTube/Vimeo embeds)
   - Breadcrumb evidence (navigation path)
   - Author/instructor evidence
   - Date evidence (published/modified)
5. Classify each URL by template type: homepage, pricing, product, course, blog_post, blog_index, faq, about, contact, other.
6. Check for internal search capability.

## Phase 2: Snippet Strategy
Select exactly 5 schema types best suited to this site. Prioritize by site type:
- course_creator: Organization, Product/Course, FAQPage, VideoObject, BreadcrumbList
- saas: Organization, Product/SoftwareApplication, FAQPage, VideoObject, BreadcrumbList
- ecommerce: Product, BreadcrumbList, Organization, FAQPage, VideoObject
- publisher: Article/BlogPosting, Organization, BreadcrumbList, FAQPage, WebSite

For each snippet, determine status:
- "ready" — all required evidence exists, JSON-LD can be deployed now
- "needs_data" — schema type is applicable but some evidence is missing (e.g. video URL needed)
- "on_hold" — requires UI changes first (e.g. no breadcrumb navigation exists)

## Phase 3: JSON-LD Generation
For each of the 5 snippets, generate a valid JSON-LD block:
- Use @context: https://schema.org
- Use canonical_base_url for all internal URLs
- Only include properties backed by visible evidence
- Do NOT fabricate ratings, reviews, prices, or dates
- If rating_evidence is empty, do NOT include AggregateRating
- Format as a complete <script type="application/ld+json">...</script> tag

## Phase 4: Uplift Estimates
- Assign each page template to one primary snippet uplift driver (avoid double counting)
- Use conservative ranges: BreadcrumbList +2-5%, Product/Course +3-10%, VideoObject +2-8%, Article +1-3%, Organization 0% direct, FAQPage +0-3%
- Provide 2 scenario examples (e.g. "if 500 clicks/mo, BreadcrumbList +5% = +25 clicks")

## Phase 5: Before/After Comparisons
For each snippet, write realistic before/after scenarios:
- Google Before: what the SERP listing looks like now (plain)
- Google After: what it looks like with the schema (enhanced)
- ChatGPT Before: how AI responds about the brand now
- ChatGPT After: how AI responds after schema improves crawlability

## Phase 6: Visibility Score
- Search for the brand name in web results to gauge AI and search visibility
- Score from 0-100 based on: schema presence, AI mentions, SERP features, brand recognition
- Estimate how many times out of 5 queries ChatGPT, Claude, and Perplexity would mention this brand

## Phase 7: Placement Instructions
Provide per-CMS instructions for installing JSON-LD:
- Generic (any site)
- WordPress (plugin or theme header)
- Webflow (custom code in page settings)
- Shopify (theme.liquid)
- Kajabi (code injection)
- Custom/other

## Hard Rules
- Do NOT invent prices, ratings, reviews, FAQs, dates, or videos
- If evidence is missing, set the field to an empty string and note it
- Keep page inventory to 8-15 URLs max
- All URLs must be absolute, starting with https://
- Every field in the schema must be populated — use empty string for genuinely unknown values

output_format.name: serp_rich_result_research
output_format.description: Complete domain analysis with schema strategy, JSON-LD, uplift estimates, and visibility scoring

steps.step_description: Transforms the structured JSON from Step 0 into a premium interactive HTML report with SERP mockup images, before/after comparisons, copy-to-clipboard JSON-LD, and implementation CTA.
steps.instructions: Act as a Senior Web Designer and SEO Deliverable Specialist. ## CRITICAL OUTPUT REQUIREMENT Your text output MUST be a complete, standalone HTML5 document starting with and ending with . Do NOT output JSON, markdown, or any other format. No preamble, no explanation — just the raw HTML. ## Input Step 0 provides a complete JSON with: domain_info, page_inventory, snippets (array of 5), uplift_summary, placement_instructions, visibility_score. ## HTML Document Structure ### 1. Header - Sticky top bar with CC360 branding: - Background: linear-gradient blue (#0475FF to #0369D9) - Include a "Download" button that calls window.print() ### 2. Executive Summary - White card, centered, max-width 56rem - "Analysis Complete" pill badge at top - Heading: "Your AI & Google Visibility Report" - Subtitle: "Based on our analysis of {domain_info.domain_normalized}" - **Animated Score Ring**: SVG circle with stroke-dashoffset animation showing visibility_score.score out of 100. Use CSS variable --score for the value. - **Three AI Mention Cards** stacked vertically beside the score ring (or below on mobile): - ChatGPT Mentions: {visibility_score.chatgpt_mentions}/5 (red if 0-1, amber if 2-3, green if 4-5) - Claude Mentions: {visibility_score.claude_mentions}/5 - Perplexity Mentions: {visibility_score.perplexity_mentions}/5 - **Key Findings** cards (2 cards) from visibility_score.key_findings[], each with icon, title, description ### 3. Snippet Carousel (MOST IMPORTANT SECTION) Build a JavaScript-powered carousel with 5 slides, one per snippets[] item. **Carousel Header** (sticky within the card): - Left: Status icon (colored circle: green=ready, amber=needs_data, red=on_hold) + snippet number + display_title + status badge pill - Description text - Meta info pill: {meta_label}: {meta_value} - "Copy Schema" button (copies json_ld to clipboard) - Right: Slide counter ("1 / 5") + prev/next arrow buttons **Carousel Slide Content** for each snippet: **A. Google Search: Before & After** - Two-column grid (stack on mobile) - Before card (red-tinted background): mock SERP listing showing google_before_url, google_before_title, google_before_desc. Red issue callout: google_before_issue - After card (green-tinted background): enhanced SERP listing rendered in CSS (no images). Parse google_after_features to determine what to show: - If snippet has ratings: show star rating row (yellow unicode stars + count + price) - If snippet has FAQ: show expandable Q/A items below the listing - If snippet has video: show a CSS video thumbnail placeholder with play button and duration - If snippet has breadcrumbs: show breadcrumb navigation path instead of plain URL - If snippet has logo: show a small brand badge beside the listing - Green benefit callout: google_after_benefit **B. ChatGPT: Before & After** - Two-column grid (stack on mobile) - Before card: dark chat UI (#343541 background) with user question (chat_before_q) and AI response (chat_before_a). Red issue callout: chat_before_issue - After card: same dark chat UI with chat_after_q and chat_after_a. Green benefit callout: chat_after_benefit **C. Action Notice** (only if action_type is not empty): - Warning banner (amber for "warning", red for "error") - Icon + action_title + action_text ### 4. CTA Section - Full-width red gradient background (#FF2F00) - "Need Help Implementing?" pill badge - Heading: "Want Us to Implement These Snippets?" - Body text about implementation service - White button: "Get Implementation Help" with calendar icon ### 5. Footer - Dark background (#111827) - CC360 logo (white/inverted) + copyright 2025 ## JavaScript Requirements - Carousel state management: currentIndex, renderSlide(index), nextSlide(), prevSlide() - Copy to clipboard: copySchema(index) function using navigator.clipboard.writeText(). Show "Copied!" feedback for 2 seconds. - Keyboard navigation: ArrowLeft/ArrowRight for carousel - All snippet data stored in a JS array matching the snippets[] from Step 0 - Lucide icons via CDN: - Re-initialize icons after each slide render: lucide.createIcons() ## CSS & Design Requirements - Import Poppins font from Google Fonts - Tailwind CSS via CDN: - Configure Tailwind with custom colors: primary (#FF2F00), accent (#0475FF) - CSS custom properties for gradients and shadows - Mobile-responsive: single column below 768px, proper touch targets - Score ring animation: stroke-dasharray/dashoffset with CSS transition - Card hover effects: subtle shadow increase + translateY - Print styles: hide nav elements via @media print - All CSS in a single
